title: The Creak
body:
The creak of the rocking chair,
a sound we all knew.
The barking of the labradors
that ran across the grass.
The snapping of branches
As we searched for the biggest sticks.
The growl of rocks
beneath our bikes.

Quiet.

No more dogs,
only the dirt where they sleep.
The chairs are gone,
Only seen by dust.
The tree snap is no more —
Replaced by the sound of our
Dreams.
Broken Dreams.

The smell of fresh cut grass is gone,
overshadowed by weeds, just like
the place where we are stuck.

The creak of the rocking chair.
The taste of iced tea
That was sweet like joy
replaced by bitterness.
Bitterness of life:
as we grow, the more it fades.
The taste, the vision, the smell—
all disappear.

But the sound
remains.

When the eyes close,
when the tears dry,
we remember the creak—
